channel.title: decorator on Python Study
channel.description: Recent content in decorator on Python Study

item.title: Function Overloading in Python
item.description: Function overloading is a mechanism in C++ that allows defining functions with the same name but different parameter sets. The compiler chooses the appropriate function based on the parameters. Each function can be adapted to work with specific parameter types. Here’s an example of declaring overloaded functions in C++: // Prototype three write functions. int write(std::string s); // Write a string. int write(double d); // Write a double. int write(double d, int p); // Write a double with a given precision.

item.title: Parameterized Decorators
item.description: In Python, decorators are a way to modify the behavior of a function without changing its source code. They are a powerful tool that can add extra functionality to your code. def add_two(func): def wrapper(*args, **kwargs): num = func(*args, **kwargs) return num + 2 return wrapper @add_two def get_num(): return 10 print(get_num()) # The result will be 12 The first example shows how you can use a simple decorator to add extra functionality to a function.
